title: Issues using generate systemd for a container that uses volumes
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

A Podman container could not automatically be started up by user's systemd.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Attempt to run the docker.io freshrss/freshrss container using this command: `podman run -d --log-opt max-size=10m   -p 44449:80   -e TZ=Europe/London   -e 'CRON_MIN=1,31'   -v freshrss_data:/var/www/FreshRSS/data   -v freshrss_extensions:/var/www/FreshRSS/extensions   --name freshrss   docker.io/freshrss/freshrss`
2. Generate a systemd file using: `podman generate systemd --new --name freshrss -f` while in `.config/systemd/user/`.
4. Enable the service using `systemctl --user enable ...`
3. Stop and remove the container `podman stop freshrss` and `podman container rm freshrss`, and then reboot.

**Describe the results you received:**

The container would keep stopping and starting constantly.

**Describe the results you expected:**

The container would start once I had logged into my user.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

When I changed the systemd generate to not use `--new`, I instead got this error: `Dec 10 11:43:25 USERNAME systemd[2513]: freshrss.service: New main PID 105697 does not belong to service, and PID file is not owned by root. Refusing.`.  

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.4.4
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.23.1
  cgroupControllers:
  - memory
  - pids
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/bin/conmon'
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.25, commit: unknown'
  cpus: 20
  distribution:
    codename: vanessa
    distribution: linuxmint
    version: "21"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: USERNAME
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.15.0-56-generic
  linkmode: dynamic
  logDriver: journald
  memFree: 6567473152
  memTotal: 33579220992
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: 'runc: /usr/sbin/runc'
    path: /usr/sbin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.1.0-0ubuntu1.1
      spec: 1.0.2-dev
      go: go1.18.1
      libseccomp: 2.5.3
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    exists: true
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
    seccompEnabled: true
    seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
    selinuxEnabled: false
  serviceIsRemote: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: 'slirp4netns: /usr/bin/slirp4netns'
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.0.1
      commit: 6a7b16babc95b6a3056b33fb45b74a6f62262dd4
      libslirp: 4.6.1
  swapFree: 2134634496
  swapTotal: 2147479552
  uptime: 11h 49m 31.14s (Approximately 0.46 days)
plugins:
  log:
  - k8s-file
  - none
  - journald
  network:
  - bridge
  - macvlan
  volume:
  - local
registries: {}
store:
  configFile: /home/USERNAME/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 2
    paused: 0
    running: 2
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
      Package: 'fuse-overlayfs: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs'
      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.10.5
        fuse-overlayfs: version 1.7.1
        FUSE library version 3.10.5
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
  graphRoot: /home/USERNAME/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: ecryptfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:
    number: 3
  runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers
  volumePath: /home/USERNAME/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 3.4.4
  Built: 0
  BuiltTime: Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.17.3
  OsArch: linux/amd64

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**

```
Listing... Done
podman/jammy,now 3.4.4+ds1-1ubuntu1 amd64 [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked [the Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)?**

Yes, I have checked the guide. I have read through the changelog and cannot see anything that mentions a fix to this issue, nor any closed issue.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Linux Mint

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.  I cannot reproduce the error but have the suspicion that the generated unit file has been edited?

Can you share the ouput of the .service file?  I think there may be a `User=` line which isn't supported.

I remember issues like this before without the `User=`, I think this is already fixed in the latest version.
@dancs-dev Please test with the latest upstream version, 3.4.4 is way to old and not something we can support upstream.

Thanks for your responses! I will aim to try with the latest upstream version (though I will need to figure out how to do that, as it is not available from the standard apt repository).

For reference, here is the output with --new:

```
# container-e1b4993ea00d1576bdcfc98b8b53842609ca44b0bb5b3d409169fc1c57498f86.service
# autogenerated by Podman 3.4.4
# Tue Dec 13 09:01:52 GMT 2022

[Unit]
Description=Podman container-e1b4993ea00d1576bdcfc98b8b53842609ca44b0bb5b3d409169fc1c57498f86.service
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=on-failure
TimeoutStopSec=70
ExecStartPre=/bin/rm -f %t/%n.ctr-id
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id --cgroups=no-conmon --rm --sdnotify=conmon --replace -d --log-opt max-size=10m -p 44449:80 -e TZ=Europe/London -e CRON_MIN=1,31 -v freshrss_data:/var/www/FreshRSS/data -v freshrss_extensions:/var/www/FreshRSS/extensions --name freshrss freshrss/freshrss
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop --ignore --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm -f --ignore --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

```

And here is the output without --new:

```
# container-e1b4993ea00d1576bdcfc98b8b53842609ca44b0bb5b3d409169fc1c57498f86.service
# autogenerated by Podman 3.4.4
# Tue Dec 13 09:03:38 GMT 2022

[Unit]
Description=Podman container-e1b4993ea00d1576bdcfc98b8b53842609ca44b0bb5b3d409169fc1c57498f86.service
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=/run/user/1000/containers

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=on-failure
TimeoutStopSec=70
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman start e1b4993ea00d1576bdcfc98b8b53842609ca44b0bb5b3d409169fc1c57498f86
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop -t 10 e1b4993ea00d1576bdcfc98b8b53842609ca44b0bb5b3d409169fc1c57498f86
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman stop -t 10 e1b4993ea00d1576bdcfc98b8b53842609ca44b0bb5b3d409169fc1c57498f86
PIDFile=/run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/e1b4993ea00d1576bdcfc98b8b53842609ca44b0bb5b3d409169fc1c57498f86/userdata/conmon.pid
Type=forking

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

```

I am not sure what is wrong with the above, but I think I have a decent workaround (is there anything wrong with this?):

```
[Unit]
Description=freshrss

[Service]
Type=simple
TimeoutStartSec=0
ExecStart=/bin/podman start freshrss
RemainAfterExit=yes
ExecStop=/bin/podman stop freshrss

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

```

The above required starting a container initially (but not removing it) and then it seems to work.

If the container is already running (and was not started by systemd) the error makes sense for units generated without --new.  Units with --new should just work.

Just to double check, @dancs-dev.  Both cases do not work, right?

> I am not sure what is wrong with the above, but I think I have a decent workaround (is there anything wrong with this?):

Only units generate by Podman are supported.  There's too much that can go wrong.

Both cases don't work unfortunately. Here is the output from the `--new` case:

```
USERNAME@HOSTNAME:~$ systemctl --user status container-freshrss.service 
○ container-freshrss.service - Podman container-freshrss.service
     Loaded: loaded (/home/USERNAME/.config/systemd/user/container-freshrss.service; enabled; vendor preset: enabled)
     Active: inactive (dead) since Thu 2022-12-15 10:03:09 GMT; 12min ago
       Docs: man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
    Process: 2359 ExecStartPre=/bin/rm -f /run/user/1000/container-freshrss.service.ctr-id (code=exited, status=0/SUC>
    Process: 2360 ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --cidfile=/run/user/1000/container-freshrss.service.ctr-id --cgroups=>
    Process: 2425 ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop --ignore --cidfile=/run/user/1000/container-freshrss.service.ctr-id (>
    Process: 2564 ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm -f --ignore --cidfile=/run/user/1000/container-freshrss.service.ctr>
   Main PID: 2360 (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
        CPU: 252ms

Dec 15 10:02:58 HOSTNAME systemd[1367]: Starting Podman container-freshrss.service...
Dec 15 10:02:58 HOSTNAME systemd[1367]: Started Podman container-freshrss.service.
lines 1-13/13 (END)
```

If I use `enable --now` and look at the status immediately, I get this:

```
● container-freshrss.service - Podman container-freshrss.service
     Loaded: loaded (/home/USERNAME/.config/systemd/user/container-freshrss.service; enabled; vendor preset: enabled)
     Active: deactivating (stop) since Thu 2022-12-15 10:22:10 GMT; 5s ago
       Docs: man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
    Process: 5808 ExecStartPre=/bin/rm -f /run/user/1000/container-freshrss.service.ctr-id (code=exited, status=0/SUC>
    Process: 5809 ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --cidfile=/run/user/1000/container-freshrss.service.ctr-id --cgroups=>
   Main PID: 5809 (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS); Control PID: 5879 (podman)
      Tasks: 0 (limit: 9239)
     Memory: 8.8M
        CPU: 120ms
     CGroup: /user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/container-freshrss.service
             ‣ 5879 /usr/bin/podman stop --ignore --cidfile=/run/user/1000/container-freshrss.service.ctr-id

```

I tried to update to the latest version, following the [Ubuntu installation guide](https://podman.io/getting-started/installation), however I got this output:

```
USERNAME@HOSTNAME:~$ sudo mkdir -p /etc/apt/keyrings
curl -fsSL https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:kubic:libcontainers:unstable/xUbuntu_$(lsb_release -rs)/Release.key \
  | gpg --dearmor \
  | sudo tee /etc/apt/keyrings/devel_kubic_libcontainers_unstable.gpg > /dev/null
echo \
  "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/devel_kubic_libcontainers_unstable.gpg]\
    https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:kubic:libcontainers:unstable/xUbuntu_$(lsb_release -rs)/ /" \
  | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:unstable.list > /dev/null
sudo apt-get update -qq
sudo apt-get -qq -y install podman
curl: (22) The requested URL returned error: 404
gpg: no valid OpenPGP data found.
E: The repository 'https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:kubic:libcontainers:unstable/xUbuntu_21  Release' does not have a Release file.
```
Thanks for coming back.  I did a fresh install of Mint and couldn't reproduce the issue.  I am a bit clueless at the moment.

Thanks for checking that, this is much appreciated! I'm not sure if this is important/useful context, but I have tried this on both my laptop and main desktop PC, and the issue occurred on both. On both machines I previously had Una (20.3) and used the Linux Mint tool to upgrade to Vanessa. I will later try on another laptop that has just Vanessa (never upgraded) and see if it works on that. Also, I don't know if this matters given that I am using `systemctl --user`, but my home partitions are encrypted.
The upgrade part is interesting.  Looking forward to hear back whether it works on a fresh install for you.  I also ran the service with `--user`.

The unit says
>  Main PID: 2360 (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)

So it sounds like the container process exited successfully?

> The unit says
> 
> > Main PID: 2360 (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
> 
> So it sounds like the container process exited successfully?

I think the behaviour is that it starts and stops straight away. Further, if I have restart always in there, the container will keep stopping/starting.

>The upgrade part is interesting. Looking forward to hear back whether it works on a fresh install for you. I also ran the service with --user.

It seems to work on my other machine with a fresh install of Vanessa! I wonder what is the cause of the problem in the upgraded version, and if it can be fixed. Would this be a Podman or Linux Mint issue?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

> I think the behaviour is that it starts and stops straight away. Further, if I have restart always in there, the container will keep stopping/starting.

That is expected.  With `always` systemd will immediately restart the container independent of the exit status.

> It seems to work on my other machine with a fresh install of Vanessa! I wonder what is the cause of the problem in the upgraded version, and if it can be fixed. Would this be a Podman or Linux Mint issue?

Hard to say as there are many moving pieces during an upgrade.  Given it works on a fresh install, I will close the issue upstream here.
